entries.definition:
ʾ–d–d abomination, hardship, disaster, affliction; to befall, to
afflict; shameful. Of this root, إِدًّا ʾiddan occurs once in the Qur’an.

إِدًّا ʾiddan [quasi-act. part.] abominable, detestable, loathsome
(19:88–9) وَقَالُوا اتَّخَذَ الرَّحْمَنُ وَلَدًا لَقَدْ جِئْتُمْ شَيْئًا إِدًّا and they say, ‘The Lord
of Mercy has begotten offspring’, how abominable is this thing
you assert.

إِدْرِيس ʾidrīs (see د/ر/س d–r–s).

آدَم ʾādam (see أ/د/م ʾ–d–m).
